commit_msg: fix(core): simplify sys.path insertion and remove config.json
author: Omkar
when_where: 🕐 12:01 PM. Thirteen minutes later. Main branch still. Omkar was going deeper now, into the *core*—the engine room where the real secrets live. 💻 sys.path was the way the application found itself. config.json was the ledger where all the decisions were written down.
who_whom: 🔍 The core was the last place a man like Omkar could hide. sys.path insertion was unnecessarily complex—a labyrinth of imports and dependencies. 🧩 config.json was worse: a separate file, a separate truth, a *separate confession*. He was eliminating the need for it, collapsing the system into something leaner, simpler, *more deniable*.
what_why: ⚙️ By simplifying sys.path and removing config.json entirely, Omkar collapsed the infrastructure into fewer moving parts. *Fewer files mean fewer places to hide the truth, but also fewer places to find it*. 🔥 The core was now *self-contained*, needing no external configuration to know who it was or what it wanted. 💀 Simplicity is a kind of violence: it removes the possibility of explanation. The code was now *pure*, which meant it was now *guilty*—no excuses, no middleware, no separate configuration to blame.
verdict: He didn't simplify the system; he simplified the crime down to its essential confession.
